rationale: Spot-checked the load-bearing Go tests by reverting the implementation in a /tmp throwaway copy (git status stayed clean in the worktree). TestWorkflowOwnsClaudeWorkflows: removing the .claude/workflows/** claim from the live OWNERSHIP.yaml makes attributeDomain return "" -> FAIL, restored -> PASS. TestClaudeSetup_InstallsWorkflowClaudeTargetOnly + the created/skipped-15 counts: removing the installWorkflows call from RunClaude fails all three (missing file, 14 vs 15 created, 14 vs 15 skipped). TestWorkflowFiles_EmbedsMsPanel: replacing WorkflowFiles() with a stale hardcoded string fails it; restored passes. TestMsPanelWorkflow_AllowedCLIExactSet: a bare 'mindspec panel create' literal inside a // comment is (correctly, per its own doc comment) NOT flagged; the same literal as a real code-level const IS flagged by the positive-enumeration scan (byte-offset error), confirming it's checking code text, not decoration. Also re-ran the RCE re-attack empirically (node harness over the extracted validators): x$(id), x;id, x|sh, x`whoami`, 'foo bar' all threw against slug/spec via validateArgs. None of these tests are vacuous. Two minor gaps, neither blocking: (1) no Go test directly byte-diffs the tracked .claude/workflows/ms-panel.js against plugins/mindspec/workflows/ms-panel.js -- only AC1's shell one-liner does, and it isn't wired into ci.yml (only `go test -short -race ./...` runs) -- but this mirrors the pre-existing skills-distribution pattern (.claude/skills/** vs plugins/mindspec/skills/** has the same gap), so it's not a regression introduced by 111. (2) installWorkflows' 'user-modified -> notice, left in place' branch has no dedicated test, mirroring installSkills' untested notice branch. R2-R5/R3b's live-agent behavior (fan-out, parse-retry ladder, quota substitution) is correctly and explicitly scoped to the spec's own disclosed Manual e2e proof, not silently untested.
findings: Confirmed by revert: TestWorkflowOwnsClaudeWorkflows fails when the .claude/workflows/** OWNERSHIP claim is removed (attributeDomain returns "").
Confirmed by revert: TestClaudeSetup_InstallsWorkflowClaudeTargetOnly and the FreshSetup/Idempotent 15-item counts all fail when the installWorkflows() call is removed from RunClaude.
Confirmed by revert: TestWorkflowFiles_EmbedsMsPanel fails when WorkflowFiles() is replaced with stale hardcoded content.

Confirmed by injection: TestMsPanelWorkflow_AllowedCLIExactSet's positive-enumeration scan catches a real code-level 'mindspec panel create' literal reused outside the ALLOWED_CLI array (fails with a byte-offset error); a mention inside a // comment is deliberately not flagged, matching the test's documented comment-skipping design -- not a bypass.

Confirmed empirically (node harness over the extracted validateArgs/validateShellSafe/validatePathElement): payloads x$(id), x;id, x|sh, x`whoami`, 'foo bar' all throw for slug and spec -- the injection surface holds.

Minor, non-blocking: no automated Go test byte-diffs the repo-tracked .claude/workflows/ms-panel.js against plugins/mindspec/workflows/ms-panel.js; that invariant (R1/R8) is enforced only by AC1's shell one-liner, which is not wired into .github/workflows/ci.yml. This mirrors the pre-existing, accepted skills-distribution pattern (.claude/skills/** vs plugins/mindspec/skills/** has the identical gap), so it is not a new regression -- but it is a real hole in the go-test-only CI net that a future PR editing one tracked copy and not the other would slip through silently.

Minor, non-blocking: installWorkflows' 'present and different -> user-modified, Notice, left in place' branch (HC-6 parity with installSkills) has no dedicated test; low risk since it mirrors already-tested installSkills logic.
